verses.text: The servants of the LORD are called to praise His name, for 'the LORD hath chosen Jacob unto himself.' Election is cause for praise. That God set His love on a people undeserving is a wonder worth singing.

'The LORD is great... above all gods,' doing as He pleases in heaven and earth, raising vapours and sending wind and rain. He governs all nature. The God who controls the weather is supreme over every power.

'The LORD is great... above all gods,' doing as He pleases in heaven and earth, raising vapours and sending wind and rain. He governs all nature. The God who controls the weather is supreme over every power.

'The LORD is great... above all gods,' doing as He pleases in heaven and earth, raising vapours and sending wind and rain. He governs all nature. The God who controls the weather is supreme over every power.
He smote Egypt's firstborn, did signs and wonders, struck great kings, and gave their land to Israel. His mighty acts delivered His people. The God of history overthrew empires to keep His promise.

'Thy name, O LORD, endureth for ever,' and He will judge His people and have compassion on His servants. His name and care never fail. The everlasting God is also the merciful Judge of His own.
'Thy name, O LORD, endureth for ever,' and He will judge His people and have compassion on His servants. His name and care never fail. The everlasting God is also the merciful Judge of His own.

The heathen's idols of silver and gold cannot speak, see, or breathe, and 'they that make them are like unto them.' Dead gods make dead worshippers. Those who trust lifeless idols become as senseless as they are.

Israel, the priests, and all who fear the LORD are called to bless Him; 'blessed be the LORD out of Zion.' The whole people join in praise. From God's dwelling rises the chorus of His grateful servants.
